calendar.matters: Motion For Trial Preference And Declaration Of Trial Preference

Motion To Set Aside Proposed Order Granting Motion To Strike, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE PROPOSED ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO STRIKE

Appearances:
Robert Stewart (415.264.4339, robertstewartphd@aol.com) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona;
Ondrej Likar and Jacqueline Yee (510.267.3000, olikar@butycurliano.com, jyee@butycurliano.com) for defendant and responding party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Robert Stewart's motion to set aside the court's prior order granting defendant's motion to strike is denied. The court construes the motion as seeking reconsideration. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 1008, subdivision (b), Stewart must show new facts, circumstances, or law that could not have previously been presented. He has not met his burden on this motion.

Defendant contends that Plaintiff's citations to Judicial Council of California v. Superior Court (2003) 29 Cal.4th 820, 829, and In re Marriage of Sherman (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 1201, 1207, are false. The citations do not return the cases Stewart has named, as Stewart concedes. The court cautions Stewart that inaccurate citations may subject him to sanctions or may give cause to the court to strike his filings. Generative AI tools that lawyers and self-represented litigants sometimes use can hallucinate case authority. Especially when using those tools, it is very important to check each case carefully to make sure that it actually exists and stands for the proposition for which it is cited.

Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Motion In Limine To Exclude Evidence Of "Professional Negligence" And "Standard Of Care" Defenses
Off Calendar Apr-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IN LIMINE TO EXCLUDE EVIDENCE OF "PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE" AND "STANDARD OF CARE" DEFENSES

Appearances:
Robert Stewart (415.264.4339) defendant appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Defendant did not give notice of his intent to contest the tentative. This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The motion was not filed or served in compliance with CCP 1005(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ERICA KURIEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Robert Stewart (415.264.4339) defendant appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Defendant did not give notice of his intent to contest the tentative. This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Erica Kurien's motion to strike the references to punitive damages from plaintiff Robert Stewart's complaint is granted without leave to amend unless and until the plaintiff obtains an order per CCP 425.13 allowing him to file an amended complaint seeking punitive damages.

Plaintiff alleges that the defendant unlawfully recorded plaintiff's confidential medical appointments without consent and maintained false documents stating that plaintiff consented to having his appointments recorded.

Defendant's motion to strike is limited to the three references to punitive damages in the complaint at paragraphs 3, 57, and subsection (d) of the prayer for relief.

Plaintiff must comply with the CCP 425.13 before seeking punitive damages because his claim "arises out of the professional negligence of a health care provider." (Central Pathology Service Med. Clinic Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181; Davis v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 623.) Plaintiff's claims are directly related to and arise out of the procedures followed by his health care provider during the course of treatment.

The court does not address plaintiff's request for trial preference in his opposition memorandum. A request for trial preference must be made by a separately filed motion and noticed to be heard in department 206. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
